status_reason: 100-step end-to-end validation of the RL-KV d20 training pipeline at production batch on 8×H100. Confirms: (1) v11 fwd CUDA + Triton bwd kernels work at production microbatch (N = device_batch × seq_len = 16 × 2048 = 32 768 per rank); (2) DDP across 8 ranks; (3) C tensor optimizer routing (20 C tensors → AdamW with c_lr=0.02); (4) checkpoint save/load schema; (5) loss decreases monotonically (10.40 → 5.12); (6) val bpb decreases (3.16 → 1.71 → 1.53). NOT a quality result — only 100 steps, ~52M tokens. Quality assessment defers to pilot grid + full d20.

training.device_batch_note: halved from 32 (MLP default) — RL-KV's autograd graph saves z[N,h] bf16 ≈ 320 MB/layer × 20 layers = 6.4 GB extra activation retention vs MLP, which makes the cross-entropy 8 GiB fp32 logits the OOM trigger at batch=32. Halving microbatch keeps total batch identical via grad_accum=2.

results.step_100_core_note: noise — only 100 steps trained; CORE@50 needs more training
results.mfu_note: MFU is reduced vs MLP baseline because grad_accum=2 halves the effective compute density per step

artifacts.next_step: Smoke C resume from step 50 → step 150 to validate checkpoint reload + DDP resume + C optimizer state continuity
